title: TIP-1.0 Capability Catalog
description: Authoritative catalog of capability labels a TokenPak-compatible component MAY publish during MCP capability negotiation or in an X-TokenPak-Capability header. TIP-reserved labels start with 'tip.'; extension labels start with 'ext.<namespace>.'. Anything else is invalid. See https://tokenpak.ai/protocol/tip-capabilities.

labels.description: Every label TIP-1.0 recognizes. Components publish a subset of these.

class.description: Behavior class per docs/protocol/terminology.md. 'required' = core profile requirement. 'profile-specific' = required only for named profiles. 'local-only' = MUST NOT cross the machine boundary. 'passthrough-sensitive' = subject to byte-fidelity rule.

labels.description: Component runs TIP-1.0 context compression before dispatch. Reduces token count without altering semantic content.
Component preserves exact request-body byte-identity on passthrough paths (no JSON re-serialization). Required where billing routing depends on body bytes (e.g. Anthropic cache_control).
Component reports provider cache hits (inferred from response metadata) separately from TokenPak cache hits and never conflates them. Required for honest cache_origin classification.
Component writes wire-side telemetry rows (one per request) to the telemetry store per the schema. Entrypoints never claim this capability — only services.telemetry_service does.
Component applies user-configured routing rules with ordered fallback chains on provider failure.
Component runs DLP/secret redaction over request bodies before dispatch. Required when the emitter claims it does not leak secrets.
Component provides local-only preview/simulation tools (compression preview, cost simulation) that never invoke a provider. Constitutes a §5.2-C pure local helper.
Component packages prompts client-side before sending (TUI/CLI pre-send optimizer).
Component manages reusable memory capsules for prompt injection.
Component maintains a local session journal of prompt-side events.
Component wires a specific client tool (Claude Code, Cursor, Aider, etc.) to a TIP-proxy.
Component exposes TokenPak to a user-space framework (LangChain, LlamaIndex, CrewAI, AutoGen).
Component registers with TokenPak's plugin system and installs hooks at documented pipeline stages.
Component reads from the telemetry store and presents derived views. No writes. No business logic beyond aggregation.
Component evaluates user-configured thresholds against telemetry data and dispatches notifications.
